info.title: LibreFang API
info.description: LibreFang Agent Operating System — REST API for managing AI agents, tools, workflows, and more.

get.summary: GET /a2a/agents — List all A2A agent cards.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/send — Submit a task to an agent via A2A.

200.description: Submit a task to an agent via A2A

get.summary: GET /a2a/tasks/{id} — Get task status from the task store.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/{id}/cancel — Cancel a tracked task.

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/agents — List discovered external A2A agents.
get.description:
Returns both `trusted` agents (approved and able to receive tasks) and
`pending` agents (discovered but not yet approved by the operator).

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/agents/{id} — Get a specific external A2A agent by index, URL, or name.

200.description: Get a specific external A2A agent

post.summary: POST /api/a2a/discover — Discover a new external A2A agent by URL.

200.description: Discover an external A2A agent by URL

post.summary: POST /api/a2a/send — Send a task to an external A2A agent.

200.description: Send a task to an external A2A agent

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/tasks/{id}/status — Get task status from an external A2A agent.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the external A2A agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents — List agents with optional filtering, pagination, and sorting.
get.description:
Query parameters (all optional — omitting them returns all agents):
  - `q`: free-text search across name and description (case-insensitive)
  - `status`: filter by lifecycle state (e.g. "running", "suspended")
  - `limit` / `offset`: pagination
  - `sort`: field to sort by — "name", "created_at", "last_active", "state"
  - `order`: "asc" (default) or "desc"

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field: name, created_at, last_active, state

post.summary: POST /api/agents — Spawn a new agent.
POST /api/agents/bulk — Create multiple agents at once.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/agents/bulk — Delete multiple agents at once.

requestBody.description: Array of agent IDs to delete

post.summary: POST /api/agents/bulk/start — Set multiple agents to Full mode.

requestBody.description: Array of agent IDs to start

200.description: Start multiple agents (set to Full mode)

post.summary: POST /api/agents/bulk/stop — Stop multiple agents' current runs.

requestBody.description: Array of agent IDs to stop

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id — Get a single agent's detailed info.

delete.description:
Idempotent (RFC 9110 §9.2.2 / §9.3.5): deleting an agent that is already
gone returns `200 OK` with `{"status": "already-deleted"}` instead of
`404`. `404` is reserved for the malformed-UUID case alone, so retried
or replayed DELETEs by clients (network blips, dashboard double-clicks)
no longer surface a phantom error. Refs #3509.

200.description: Agent killed (or was already absent — idempotent)

409.description: Agent is hand-owned and cannot be deleted directly

200.description: Partially update an agent (name, description, model, system prompt)

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/clone — Clone an agent with its workspace files.

requestBody.description: New name for the cloned agent

200.description: Clone an agent with its workspace files

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/config — Hot-update agent name, description, system prompt, and identity.

200.description: Hot-update agent name, description, system prompt, identity, and model

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/deliveries — List recent delivery receipts for an agent.

200.description: List recent delivery receipts for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/events — Recent turn-level events for one agent.
get.description:
Backs the dashboard's agent-detail Logs tab. Returns rows sourced
from `usage_events` (newest first) so the panel shows real
operational data — model dispatch, latency, tokens, cost — instead
of the audit ledger, which is mostly admin lifecycle entries.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/files — List workspace identity files.

200.description: List workspace identity files for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Read a workspace identity file.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Write a workspace identity file.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Delete a workspace identity file.

DELETE /api/agents/{id}/hand-runtime-config — Drop all runtime overrides
for the hand agent's role, restoring the live manifest to the HAND.toml
defaults and persisting the cleared state to `hand_state.json`.
delete.description:
Returns 204 No Content on success (idempotent — a second call against an
already-clean role is also 204).

204.description: Runtime overrides cleared; manifest restored to HAND.toml defaults

400.description: Invalid agent id or target agent is not managed by a hand

409.description: Hand role not found for the agent (hand registry inconsistency)

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/hand-runtime-config — Runtime-only config override for hand agents.

requestBody.description: Runtime override fields. Whitespace is trimmed on all string fields. For `model` and `provider` an empty (or whitespace-only) string is ignored ('leave unchanged'); for the nullable secrets `api_key_env` and `base_url` an empty (or whitespace-only) string clears the override.

200.description: Runtime override applied to the live manifest and persisted to hand_state.json

400.description: Invalid agent id or target agent is not managed by a hand

409.description: Hand role not found for the agent (hand registry inconsistency)

delete.summary: DELETE /api/agents/{id}/history — Clear ALL conversation history for an agent.

200.description: Clear all conversation history for an agent

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/identity — Update an agent's visual identity.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/:id/inject — Inject a message into a running agent's tool loop.
post.description:
If the agent is currently executing tools (mid-turn), the injected message
will be processed between tool calls, interrupting the remaining sequence.
Returns `{"injected": true}` if accepted, `{"injected": false}` if no
active tool loop is running for this agent.

503.description: All injection channels for the agent are full; retry shortly (#3575)

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/logs — Returns structured execution logs for an agent.
get.description:
Supports optional query parameters:
- `n`: max number of log entries (default 100, max 1000)
- `level`: filter by outcome (e.g. "error", "ok")
- `offset`: number of matching entries to skip for pagination (default 0)

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/mcp_servers — Get an agent's MCP server assignment info.

200.description: Get an agent's MCP server assignment info

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/mcp_servers — Update an agent's MCP server allowlist.

200.description: Update an agent's MCP server allowlist

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/memory/export — Export all KV memory for an agent as JSON.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/:id/memory/import — Import KV memory from JSON into an agent.
post.description:
Accepts a JSON body with a `kv` object mapping string keys to JSON values.
Optionally accepts `clear_existing: true` to wipe existing memory before import.

**Response contract — clients MUST inspect `body.status`, not just the
HTTP status code.** A 200 may indicate either:
  - `{ "status": "imported", "keys_imported": N }` — every key written.
  - `{ "status": "partial", "keys_imported": N, "failed_keys": [...] }` —
    one or more keys failed at the substrate layer; the rest were
    written. The endpoint deliberately does not surface partial as
    207 Multi-Status to avoid breaking existing callers that gate on
    `status == 200`. Treat any non-`"imported"` body status as a
    soft failure that requires retrying the listed keys.

200.description: Memory imported (status="imported") OR partial failure (status="partial" with failed_keys list — clients must check body)

404.description: Agent not found, or caller is not the agent's author and not an admin

500.description: Backend failure clearing existing memory before import

post.summary: POST /api/agents/:id/message — Send a message to an agent.
POST /api/agents/:id/message/stream — SSE streaming response.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/metrics — Returns aggregated metrics for an agent.
get.description:
Includes message count, token usage, tool execution count, error count,
average response time (estimated), and cost data.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/:id/mode — Change an agent's operational mode.

post.summary:
`POST /api/agents/:id/push` — push a proactive outbound message from an
agent to a channel recipient (e.g., Telegram chat, Slack channel, email).
post.description:
The agent must exist, but the message is sent directly through the channel
adapter without going through the agent loop. This is the REST API
counterpart of the built-in `channel_send` tool that agents can self-invoke.

400.description: Invalid agent ID or missing required fields

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/reload — Re-read the agent's agent.toml from disk.
post.description:
Picks up manual edits to fields like `skills`, `mcp_servers`, `tools`,
or `system_prompt` without restarting the daemon. Runtime-only fields
(workspace path, tags) are preserved.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/runtime — Snapshot of in-flight loops for the agent.
get.description:
Returns one entry per `(agent, session)` pair that's currently executing.
Empty array when the agent is idle.

200.description: List of in-flight sessions for the agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/session — Get agent session (conversation history).

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Optional session id to load instead of the canonical active session

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/session/compact — Trigger LLM session compaction.
POST /api/agents/{id}/session/reboot — Hard-reboot an agent's session (full clear, no summary).

200.description: Hard-reboot an agent's session without saving summary

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/session/reset — Reset an agent's session.

200.description: List all sessions for an agent

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions — Create a new session for an agent.

requestBody.description: Optional label for the new session

200.description: Create a new session for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/by-label/:label — Find session by label (scoped to agent).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions/import — Import a previously exported session.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/export — Export a session for hibernation.

post.summary:
POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/stop — Cancel a single
in-flight `(agent, session)` loop without affecting the agent's other
concurrent sessions.
post.description:
Returns `{"status":"ok","stopped":true}` when a loop was running for that
pair, `{"status":"ok","stopped":false}` when nothing was running (already
finished, never started, or the session belongs to a different agent).

get.summary:
GET /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/stream — attach to a session's
in-flight stream events (SSE).
get.description:
Any client can subscribe to the events emitted by an active turn on this
session: the originating client (CLI, Tauri desktop, web) plus any number
of additional clients. Late attachers begin receiving events from the
moment they subscribe — partial-turn snapshots are not replayed.

Returns 404 if the session does not exist or belongs to a different agent.

200.description: Server-sent events stream of session events

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/switch — Switch to an existing session.

get.summary:
GET /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/trajectory — Export a redacted
trajectory (audit trail) for the given session.
get.description:
Returns a privacy-redacted bundle of the session messages plus metadata
(agent name, model, system prompt fingerprint, librefang version). Intended
for support, audit, and compliance flows.

Query parameters:
- `format=json` (default): single JSON object response.
- `format=jsonl`: NDJSON, first line is metadata header, subsequent lines
  are messages one-per-line. `Content-Type: application/x-ndjson`.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/skills — Get an agent's skill assignment info.

200.description: Get an agent's skill assignment info

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/skills — Update an agent's skill allowlist.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/stats — 24-hour KPI rollup for one agent.
get.description:
Returns sessions/cost/P95-latency/active-now in a single round trip so
the dashboard's per-agent KPI tiles don't have to scan the global
`/api/sessions` page (which is paginated and was clipping data for
agents that hadn't appeared in the latest N sessions).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/stop — Cancel an agent's current LLM run.

200.description: Cancel an agent's current LLM run

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/:id/suspend — Suspend an agent (stops cron, keeps in registry).

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/tools — Get an agent's tool allowlist/blocklist.

200.description: Get an agent's tool allowlist and blocklist

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/tools — Update an agent's tool allowlist/blocklist.

200.description: Update an agent's tool allowlist and blocklist

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/traces — Get decision traces from the agent's most recent message.
get.description:
Returns structured traces showing why each tool was selected during the last
agent loop execution. Useful for debugging, auditing, and optimization.

200.description: Get decision traces from the agent's most recent message

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/upload — Upload a file attachment.
post.description:
Accepts raw body bytes. The client must set:
- `Content-Type` header (e.g., `image/png`, `text/plain`, `application/pdf`)
- `X-Filename` header (original filename)

200.description: Upload a file attachment for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/approvals — List pending and recent approval requests.
get.description:
Transforms field names to match the dashboard template expectations:
`action_summary` → `action`, `agent_id` → `agent_name`, `requested_at` → `created_at`.

200.description: Paginated list of pending and recent approvals

get.summary: GET /api/approvals/{id} — Get a single approval request by ID.

post.summary: POST /api/approvals/{id}/reject — Reject a pending request.

get.summary: GET /api/audit/query — admin-only filtered audit log.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Filter by user id (UUID) or name

get.summary: GET /api/audit/recent — Get recent audit log entries.
GET /api/audit/verify — Verify the audit chain integrity.
GET /api/auth/callback — Handle the OAuth2 authorization code callback (browser redirect).

post.summary: POST /api/auth/callback — Handle the OAuth2 callback (programmatic clients).
Change the dashboard password and/or username.
post.description:
Verifies the current password, then updates whichever credentials are
provided in the request body. At least one of `new_password` or
`new_username` must be non-empty. All existing sessions are invalidated on success.

200.description: Credentials updated and existing sessions invalidated

400.description: Missing required fields or password too short

get.summary: Check what auth mode the dashboard needs.

200.description: Auth mode for the dashboard SPA — one of `none`, `api_key`, `credentials`, or `hybrid`

post.summary:
Dashboard credential login — validates username/password using Argon2id
(with transparent fallback from legacy plaintext passwords) and returns
a randomly generated session token with expiration metadata.

200.description: Login outcome — returns session token on success or `requires_totp` when 2FA is needed

401.description: Invalid username, password, or TOTP code

post.summary: POST /api/auth/introspect — Validate a token and return its claims.
post.description: Follows RFC 7662 conventions: returns `{"active": true/false, ...}`.

get.summary: GET /api/auth/login — Redirect to the external identity provider (legacy single-provider).
GET /api/auth/login/:provider — Redirect to a specific provider.

post.summary: Invalidate the caller's dashboard session and clear the browser cookie.
post.description:
Accepts the token via the `librefang_session` cookie, `Authorization:
Bearer ...`, or `X-API-Key`. Always clears the cookie client-side so a
caller who already lost their token can still wipe it locally.

get.summary: GET /api/auth/providers — List available authentication providers.

post.summary: POST /api/auth/refresh — Exchange a refresh token for a new access token.
post.description:
When the access token expires, clients can call this endpoint with the
refresh token received during login instead of forcing a full re-authorization.
If the request body omits `refresh_token`, the server looks up the token store
for a previously stored refresh token (from the OAuth callback).

get.summary: GET /api/auth/userinfo — Return info about the currently authenticated user.
get.description:
If a valid JWT is in the Authorization header and JWKS validation succeeds,
returns the decoded claims. Otherwise falls back to provider userinfo endpoint.

post.summary: POST /api/backup — Create a backup archive of kernel state.
post.description:
Returns the backup metadata including the filename. The archive is stored
in `<home_dir>/backups/` with a timestamped filename.

get.summary: GET /api/backups — List existing backups.
GET /api/bindings — List all agent bindings.

post.summary: POST /api/bindings — Add a new agent binding.
post.description:
Validation is **advisory**: a binding referencing an unknown agent is
still accepted (kernel-side `add_binding` is infallible and binding
state may be primed before the target agent spawns), but a `WARN` is
logged so misconfiguration is visible. Both name lookup and UUID
resolution check the registry — the previous form passed any
well-formed UUID even if no such agent existed, so the warn would
never fire on a UUID typo.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/bindings/:index — Remove a binding by index.

get.summary: GET /api/budget — Current budget status (limits, spend, % used).

put.summary: PUT /api/budget — Update global budget limits (in-memory only, not persisted to config.toml).

requestBody.description: Partial budget config (max_hourly_usd / max_daily_usd / max_monthly_usd / alert_threshold / default_max_llm_tokens_per_hour)

get.summary: GET /api/budget/agents — Per-agent cost ranking (top spenders).
get.description:
Uses a single `GROUP BY agent_id` query instead of one `SUM` per agent to
eliminate the N+1 SQLite pattern that caused ~1200 queries/min under normal
dashboard polling at 100 agents. See #3684.

Envelope is the canonical `PaginatedResponse{items,total,offset,limit}` per
#3842; the underlying GROUP BY returns the full ranking in a single shot, so
`offset=0`, `limit=None`.

get.summary: GET /api/budget/agents/{id} — Per-agent budget/quota status.

put.summary: PUT /api/budget/agents/{id} — Update per-agent budget limits at runtime.

200.description: Updated agent ResourceQuota (max_cost_per_hour_usd, max_cost_per_day_usd, max_cost_per_month_usd, max_llm_tokens_per_hour, …)

get.summary: GET /api/budget/users — admin-only per-user spend ranking.
get.description:
Query params:
  - `limit` (default 25, hard cap 1000) — max rows returned.

Response: `{ "users": [...], "total": N }`. Each row carries the
rolled-up hourly / daily / monthly cost plus the per-user budget
limits (when configured) so the dashboard can render % bars without
a follow-up call.

get.summary: GET /api/budget/users/{user_id} — admin-only single-user budget detail.
get.description:
`user_id` accepts either a UUID (the canonical `UserId` form) or the
raw configured name (re-derived via `UserId::from_name`) so operators
can paste a name from `config.toml` directly into the URL.

get.summary: GET /api/catalog/status — Check last catalog sync time.

post.summary: POST /api/catalog/update — Sync model catalog from the remote repository.
post.description:
Downloads the latest catalog TOML files from GitHub and caches them locally.
After syncing, the kernel's in-memory catalog is refreshed.

get.summary: GET /api/channels — List all 40 channel adapters with status and field metadata.
get.description:
Envelope is the canonical `PaginatedResponse{items,total,offset,limit}`
shape used by `/api/agents`, `/api/peers`, `/api/skills`, etc. (#3842).
The full channel registry is materialized in-memory, so this is a single
page — `offset=0`, `limit=None`. The bespoke `configured_count` sibling
is preserved for the dashboard's "X of Y configured" sub-line.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/reload — Manually trigger a channel hot-reload from disk config.
POST /api/channels/wechat/qr/start — Request a QR code from iLink for WeChat login.

get.summary: GET /api/channels/wechat/qr/status — Poll iLink for QR scan confirmation.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/whatsapp/qr/start — Start a WhatsApp Web QR login session.
post.description:
If a WhatsApp Web gateway is available (e.g. a Baileys-based bridge process),
this proxies the request and returns a base64 QR code data URL. If no gateway
is running, it returns instructions to set one up.

get.summary: GET /api/channels/whatsapp/qr/status — Poll for QR scan completion.
get.description:
After calling `/qr/start`, the frontend polls this to check if the user
has scanned the QR code and the WhatsApp Web session is connected.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/{name}/configure — Save channel secrets + config fields.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/channels/{name}/configure — Remove channel secrets + config section.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/{name}/test — Connectivity check + optional live test message.
post.description:
Accepts an optional JSON body with `channel_id` (for Discord/Slack) or `chat_id`
(for Telegram). When provided, sends a real test message to verify the bot can
post to that channel.

Status code semantics (#3507, #3505):
- `200 OK` — credentials present (and, when a target was given, message sent);
  body uses the legacy `{"status": "ok", "message": …}` shape.
- `404 Not Found` — unknown channel name; body uses `ApiErrorResponse`
  (`{"error": "Unknown channel"}`).
- `412 Precondition Failed` — required env vars / credentials are missing;
  body uses `ApiErrorResponse` (`{"error": "Missing required env vars: …"}`).
- `502 Bad Gateway` — credentials valid but downstream send failed;
  body uses `ApiErrorResponse`.

`fetch().ok` is the source of truth. Error bodies were migrated from the
ad-hoc `{"status": "error", "message": …}` shape to the canonical
`ApiErrorResponse` envelope as part of #3505 so clients have a single
parsing strategy across the API surface.

requestBody.description: Optional target — `{ "channel_id": "..." }` for Discord/Slack or `{ "chat_id": "..." }` for Telegram

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/browse — Browse ClawHub skills by sort order.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `sort` — sort order: "trending", "downloads", "stars", "updated", "rating" (default: "trending")
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)
- `cursor` — pagination cursor from previous response

200.description: Browse ClawHub skills by sort order

post.summary: POST /api/clawhub/install — Install a skill from ClawHub.
post.description:
Runs the full security pipeline: SHA256 verification, format detection,
manifest security scan, prompt injection scan, and binary dependency check.

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/search — Search ClawHub skills using vector/semantic search.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `q` — search query (required)
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/skill/{slug} — Get detailed info about a ClawHub skill.

200.description: Get detailed info about a ClawHub skill

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/skill/{slug}/code — Fetch the source code (SKILL.md) of a ClawHub skill.

200.description: Fetch source code of a ClawHub skill

get.summary: GET /api/commands — List available chat commands (for dynamic slash menu).
GET /api/commands/{name} — Lookup a single command by name.
GET /api/comms/events — Return recent inter-agent communication events.
get.description:
Sources from both the event bus (for lifecycle events with full context)
and the audit log (for message/spawn/kill events that are always captured).

Envelope is the canonical `PaginatedResponse{items,total,offset,limit}`
shape used by `/api/agents` (#3842). Events are returned in a single
page capped by `limit` (default 100, max 500); `offset` is always 0.

get.summary: GET /api/comms/events/stream — SSE stream of inter-agent communication events.
get.description: Polls the audit log every 500ms for new inter-agent events.

post.summary: POST /api/comms/send — Send a message from one agent to another.
POST /api/comms/task — Post a task to the agent task queue.

200.description: Post a task to the agent task queue

get.summary: GET /api/comms/topology — Build agent topology graph from registry.
GET /api/config — Get kernel configuration (secrets redacted).

post.summary: POST /api/config/reload — Reload configuration from disk and apply hot-reloadable changes.
post.description:
Reads the config file, diffs against current config, validates the new config,
and applies hot-reloadable actions (approval policy, cron limits, etc.).
Returns the reload plan showing what changed and what was applied.

get.summary: GET /api/config/schema — Return a simplified JSON description of the config structure.

post.summary: POST /api/config/set — Set a single config value and persist to config.toml.
post.description:
Accepts JSON `{ "path": "section.key", "value": "..." }`.
Writes the value to the TOML config file and triggers a reload.

200.description: Set a single config value and persist

get.summary: GET /api/cron/jobs — List all cron jobs, optionally filtered by agent_id.

post.summary: POST /api/cron/jobs — Create a new cron job.

put.summary: PUT /api/cron/jobs/{id} — Update a cron job's configuration.
put.description:
Returns 500 if the in-memory update succeeds but persistence to disk
fails — without persistence, the new schedule runs in-memory until the
next restart, then silently reverts to the old schedule (issue #3515).

500.description: Persist failed; change will not survive restart

delete.description:
Idempotent (RFC 9110 §9.2.2): deleting a cron job that is already gone
returns `200 OK` with `{"status": "already-deleted"}` instead of `404`.
`400` is reserved for the malformed-UUID case alone (Refs #3509). Returns
`500` if the in-memory removal succeeds but persistence to disk fails —
without persistence, the deletion would silently revert on daemon restart
(issue #3515).

200.description: Cron job deleted (or was already absent — idempotent)

500.description: Persist failed; change will not survive restart

put.summary: PUT /api/cron/jobs/{id}/enable — Enable or disable a cron job.
put.description:
Returns 500 if the in-memory toggle succeeds but persistence to disk
fails — without persistence, the new enabled state would silently
revert on daemon restart (issue #3515).

500.description: Persist failed; change will not survive restart

get.summary: GET /api/cron/jobs/{id}/status — Get status of a specific cron job.
get.description:
Same response shape as `GET /api/cron/jobs/{id}`, including the
#3693 `session_message_count` / `session_token_count` fields.

get.summary: GET /api/extensions — List catalog entries annotated with installed state.

200.description: List catalog entries with install/health status

post.summary:
POST /api/extensions/install — Install a catalog entry (alias for
POST /api/mcp/servers with template_id).
POST /api/extensions/uninstall — Uninstall by catalog id (template_id).

get.summary: GET /api/extensions/:name — Get details for a single catalog entry.
GET /api/hands — List all hand definitions (marketplace).
GET /api/hands/active — List active hand instances.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/install — Install a hand from TOML content.

200.description: Install a hand from TOML content

get.summary: GET /api/hands/instances/{id}/browser — Get live browser state for a hand instance.

200.description: Get live browser state for a hand instance

post.summary: POST /api/hands/instances/{id}/pause — Pause a hand instance.
POST /api/hands/instances/{id}/resume — Resume a paused hand instance.

get.summary: GET /api/hands/instances/{id}/stats — Get dashboard stats for a hand instance.

get.summary:
GET /api/health — Minimal liveness probe (public, no auth required).
Returns only status and version to prevent information leakage.
Use GET /api/health/detail for full diagnostics (requires auth).
GET /api/health/detail — Full health diagnostics (requires auth).

post.summary: POST /hooks/agent — Run an isolated agent turn via webhook.
post.description:
Sends a message directly to the specified agent and returns the response.
This enables external systems (CI/CD, Slack, etc.) to trigger agent work.

Auth (#3509): missing or invalid bearer token returns `401 Unauthorized`
with a `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="librefang-webhook"` header per
RFC 9110 §11.6.1, mirroring the `/hooks/wake` fix.

404.description: Webhook triggers not enabled or agent not found

post.summary: POST /hooks/wake — Inject a system event via webhook trigger.
post.description:
Publishes a custom event through the kernel's event system, which can
trigger proactive agents that subscribe to the event type.

Auth (#3509): missing or invalid bearer token returns `401 Unauthorized`
with a `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="librefang-webhook"` header per
RFC 9110 §11.6.1. The previous behaviour (400 Bad Request) confused
clients that tried to retry with a fixed body instead of fixing the
token.

post.summary: POST /api/init — Quick initialization (detect provider, write config, reload).
post.description: Skips if config.toml already exists. Returns the detected provider/model.

get.summary: GET /api/logs/stream — SSE endpoint for real-time audit log streaming.
get.description:
Streams new audit entries as Server-Sent Events. Accepts optional query
parameters for filtering:
  - `level`  — filter by classified level (info, warn, error)
  - `filter` — text substring filter across action/detail/agent_id

A heartbeat ping is sent every 15 seconds to keep the connection alive.
The endpoint polls the audit log every second. The first poll sends up
to the most recent 200 entries as a bounded backfill (prevents a flood
when subscribing against a long-running daemon); every subsequent poll
uses a cursor (`since_seq`) so bursts faster than the previous
`recent(200)` sliding window are no longer silently truncated.

get.summary: GET /api/marketplace/search — Search the FangHub marketplace.
GET /api/mcp/catalog — List all installable MCP catalog entries.
GET /api/mcp/catalog/{id} — Single catalog entry detail.

200.description: Health snapshot for all configured MCP servers

post.description:

get.summary: GET /api/mcp/taint-rules — List configured `[[taint_rules]]`.
get.description:
Issue #3050 follow-up: the dashboard `TaintPolicyEditor` references
rule-set names by free-form string. Without this read-only endpoint,
the editor cannot tell the operator that a typed name doesn't match
any registered set — and the scanner silently treats unknown names
as no-ops (one-shot WARN in
`librefang_runtime_mcp::warn_unknown_rule_set_once`). The dashboard
uses this list to render an inline validation hint next to the
`rule_sets` field.

200.description: List configured named taint rule sets

get.summary: List all proactive memories, optionally filtered by category, with pagination.
get.description:
When proactive memory is disabled in config, returns an empty list with
`proactive_enabled: false` (HTTP 200) so the dashboard can render an
explanatory note instead of treating a config state as a server error.

post.summary: Clean up expired session-level memories across all agents.
post.description:
Deletes session memories older than `session_ttl_hours` (default 24).
Only session-level memories are affected — user and agent memories are persistent.

Search proactive memories by semantic similarity.

When proactive memory is disabled, returns `{stats: null, proactive_enabled: false}`
at HTTP 200 — disabled is a config state, not an error.

get.summary: Get all memories for a specific user.
GET /api/metrics — Prometheus text-format metrics.
get.description:
Returns counters and gauges for monitoring LibreFang in production:
- `librefang_agents_active` — number of active agents
- `librefang_uptime_seconds` — seconds since daemon started
- `librefang_tokens` — total tokens consumed (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_tokens_input` — input tokens consumed (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_tokens_output` — output tokens consumed (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_tool_calls` — tool calls made (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_llm_calls` — LLM API calls made (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_panics_total` — supervisor panic count
- `librefang_restarts_total` — supervisor restart count
- `librefang_active_sessions` — number of active login sessions
- `librefang_cost_usd_today` — total estimated cost for today (USD)
- `librefang_http_requests_total` — HTTP request counts (with telemetry feature)
- `librefang_http_request_duration_seconds` — HTTP request latencies (with telemetry feature)

post.summary: POST /api/migrate — Run migration from another agent framework.

GET /api/providers — List all providers with auth status.
get.description:
For local providers (ollama, vllm, lmstudio), also probes reachability and
discovers available models via their health endpoints.

Probes run **concurrently** and results are **cached for 60 seconds** so the
endpoint responds instantly on repeated dashboard loads even when local
services are offline.

get.summary: GET /api/providers/github-copilot/oauth/poll/{poll_id}
get.description:
Poll the status of a GitHub device flow.
Returns `pending`, `complete`, `expired`, `denied`, or `error`.
On `complete`, saves the token to secrets.env and sets GITHUB_TOKEN.

Initiates a GitHub device flow for Copilot authentication.

get.summary: GET /api/providers/{name} — Get details for a single provider.

Looks up the best default model for the given provider and updates both
the in-memory override and config.toml so it persists across restarts.

requestBody.description: Optional `{ "model": "model-id" }` to override the auto-selected default

delete.summary: DELETE /api/providers/{name}/key — Remove an API key for a provider.

put.summary: PUT /api/providers/{name}/url — Set a custom base URL for a provider.

get.summary: GET /api/queue/status — Command queue status and occupancy.

post.summary: POST /api/restore — Restore kernel state from a backup archive.
post.description:
Accepts a JSON body with `{"filename": "librefang_backup_20260315_120000.zip"}`.
The file must exist in `<home_dir>/backups/`.

**Warning**: This overwrites existing state files. The daemon should be
restarted after a restore for all changes to take effect.

get.summary: GET /api/schedules — List all scheduled jobs.
get.description:
Envelope is the canonical `PaginatedResponse{items,total,offset,limit}`
(#3842) so the generated SDK can share one list-response type across all
list endpoints. The legacy `schedules` key was renamed to `items`; offset
is always 0 and limit is null because this endpoint returns the full set.

post.summary: POST /api/skills/install — Install a skill from FangHub (GitHub).

get.summary: GET /api/templates — List available agent templates.
GET /api/templates/:name — Get template details.
GET /api/tools — List all tool definitions (built-in + MCP).
GET /api/tools/:name — Get a single tool definition by name.

post.summary: POST /api/tools/{name}/invoke — Invoke a kernel tool directly.
post.description:
External integrations (MCP bridges, scripts, automations) can call kernel
tools without going through an agent loop. Fail-closed: the endpoint
rejects every request unless the tool is listed in
`[tool_invoke] allowlist` and `tool_invoke.enabled = true`. Pass
`?agent_id=<uuid>` when invoking approval-gated tools so the approval
callback can resolve the correct agent; without an `agent_id` those
tools are rejected to avoid orphaned deferred executions.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Caller agent UUID (required for approval-gated tools)

400.description: Tool invocation failed or requires an agent context

403.description: Endpoint disabled or tool not in allowlist

post.summary: POST /api/triggers — Register a new event trigger.

get.summary: GET /api/triggers/:id — Fetch a single trigger by ID.

delete.description:
Idempotent (RFC 9110 §9.2.2): deleting a trigger that is already gone
returns `200 OK` with `{"status": "already-deleted"}` instead of `404`.
`400` is reserved for the malformed-UUID case alone. Refs #3509.

200.description: Trigger deleted (or was already absent — idempotent)

patch.description:
All body fields are optional. Only provided fields are changed.
Supported fields: `pattern`, `prompt_template`, `enabled`, `max_fires`,
`cooldown_secs` (pass `null` to clear), `session_mode` (pass `null` to clear),
`target_agent_id` (pass `null` to clear, omit to leave unchanged).

requestBody.description: Partial trigger fields: pattern, prompt_template, enabled, max_fires, cooldown_secs, session_mode, target_agent_id

get.summary: GET /api/uploads/{file_id} — Serve an uploaded file.

200.description: Serve an uploaded file by ID

get.summary: GET /api/usage — Get per-agent usage statistics.
get.description:
The per-agent rollup is materialized from the in-memory agent registry
and returned in one page — `offset=0` and `limit=None` always.

get.summary: GET /api/usage/by-model — Get usage grouped by model.
GET /api/usage/daily — Get daily usage breakdown for the last 7 days.
GET /api/usage/summary — Get overall usage summary from UsageStore.

post.summary: Rotate a user's API key.
post.description:
Generates a fresh 32-byte random plaintext key, hashes it with Argon2id,
stores the hash in `config.toml`, and **swaps the live in-memory snapshot
the auth middleware reads from** so the next request that presents the
old plaintext token immediately fails authentication. Without the live
swap a leaked key could only be revoked by restarting the daemon — which
defeats the point of rotation. See the `user_api_keys` field on
[`AppState`] for the shared `Arc<RwLock<…>>` that makes this work.

Owner-only, gated by `is_owner_only_write` in `middleware.rs` — same
blast radius as user create/delete. The request takes no body; the new
plaintext key is returned in the response and is **never written to the
audit log or echoed again**.

Dashboard sessions (`AppState.active_sessions`) are intentionally NOT
invalidated here. The active-sessions store is keyed by an opaque token
and the stored [`crate::password_hash::SessionToken`] does not carry a
`user_id` — it tracks the single shared dashboard credential pair
(`dashboard_user` / `dashboard_pass`), not the per-user API keys this
endpoint rotates. The two auth surfaces are independent: a per-user
bearer-token caller never lands in `active_sessions` at all (see
`middleware.rs:618`), so the per-user key swap completes the kill on
its own. If a future change ties dashboard sessions to a `UserId`, we
can extend `sessions_invalidated` to include those evictions; today
the count reflects the per-user-bearer-token kill, which is the actual
revocation that matters for this surface.

200.description: Key rotated. `new_api_key` is the only time the plaintext is exposed — the server cannot reproduce it later.

get.summary: GET /api/version — Build & version info (includes API versioning).
GET /api/versions — List supported API versions and negotiation info.
GET /api/workflows — List all workflows.

post.summary: POST /api/workflows — Register a new workflow.
POST /api/workflows/:id/run — Execute a workflow.

get.summary: GET /api/workflows/:id/runs — List runs for a workflow.

post.summary: POST /api/workflows/:id/save-as-template — Convert a workflow into a reusable template.
POST /mcp — Handle MCP JSON-RPC requests over HTTP.
post.description:
Exposes the same MCP protocol normally served via stdio, allowing
external MCP clients to connect over HTTP instead.

200.description: Handle MCP JSON-RPC requests over HTTP

post.summary: POST /v1/chat/completions

get.summary: GET /v1/models — List available agents as OpenAI model objects.

AgentEventRowView.description:
Wire-shape for one row in [`list_agent_events`]. Mirrors
[`librefang_memory::usage::AgentEventRow`] but defined here as a
utoipa::ToSchema view so we can register it with the OpenAPI doc
without forcing utoipa into the memory crate.

AgentStats24hView.description:
24-hour KPI rollup view returned by `GET /api/agents/{id}/stats`.
Mirrors [`librefang_memory::session::AgentStats24h`] — defined here as a
view so we can derive `utoipa::ToSchema` without forcing utoipa into the
memory crate. Generated SDKs and the OpenAPI spec pick up this shape.

AgentStatsPrevView.description: Prior 24-48h window scoped fields backing the KPI tile trend deltas.

AttachmentRef.description: A file attachment reference (from a prior upload).

BulkActionResult.description: Outcome of a single bulk action (delete/start/stop).

BulkAgentIdsRequest.description: Request containing a list of agent IDs for bulk operations (delete/start/stop).

BulkCreateRequest.description: Request to create multiple agents at once.

BulkCreateResult.description: Outcome of a single bulk-create item.

BulkImportRequest.description:
Bulk-import payload. `rows` are pre-parsed by the frontend (drag-drop
CSV, dialect-aware). `dry_run = true` returns counts without persisting.

CallbackBody.description: POST body for the callback (programmatic clients).

state.description: State parameter for CSRF validation (signed token).

new_password.description: New password — optional, omit to keep the current password.

new_username.description: New username — optional, omit to keep the current username.

ClawHubInstallRequest.description: Request to install a skill from ClawHub.

hand.description: Install into a specific hand's workspace instead of globally.

CloneAgentRequest.description: Request body for cloning an agent.

include_skills.description: Whether to copy skills from the source agent (default: true).

include_tools.description: Whether to copy tools from the source agent (default: true).

InjectMessageRequest.description: Request to inject a message into a running agent's tool-execution loop (#956).

message.description: The message to inject between tool calls.

session_id.description: Optional session id; when omitted the message broadcasts to all live sessions for the agent.

InjectMessageResponse.description: Response from a mid-turn message injection.

injected.description: Whether the message was accepted (true = injected, false = no active loop).

MessageRequest.description: Request to send a message to an agent.

ephemeral.description:
If true, this is an ephemeral "side question" (`/btw`).
The message is answered using the agent's system prompt but WITHOUT
loading or saving session history — the real conversation is untouched.

group_participants.description:
Optional group participant roster (Phase 2 §C addressee guard).

Forwarded by the WhatsApp gateway for group messages so the kernel's
addressee guard (`is_addressed_to_other_participant`) can detect when
a turn is addressed to a named participant other than the agent.

`#[serde(default)]` ensures backward compatibility for callers (Telegram,
direct API) that don't populate this field.

is_group.description: Whether this message originated from a group chat (vs DM).

session_id.description:
Optional explicit session ID (UUID string) to use for this message.

When set, overrides the default session resolution (channel-derived or
registry canonical). Enables multi-tab / multi-session UIs where the
caller tracks which session each conversation belongs to.

Safety: the server rejects a `session_id` that belongs to a different
agent with 400 Bad Request.

show_thinking.description:
Whether the response should include the model's thinking/reasoning trace.

`None` defaults to `true` when thinking content is available.

thinking.description:
Per-call deep-thinking override.

- `Some(true)`: force thinking on (even if the manifest has it off)
- `Some(false)`: force thinking off (even if the manifest has it on)
- `None`: use the manifest/global default

was_mentioned.description: Whether the bot was @mentioned in a group message.

decision_traces.description:
Decision traces from tool calls made during the agent loop.
Empty if no tools were called.

memories_saved.description:
Summaries of memories that were saved during this turn.
Empty when no new memories were extracted.

memories_used.description:
Summaries of memories that were recalled and used as context.
Empty when no relevant memories were found.

memory_conflicts.description:
Detected memory conflicts where new info contradicts existing memories.
Empty when no conflicts were detected.

owner_notice.description:
§A — Optional private notice destined for the agent's owner DM,
produced when the model invoked the `notify_owner` tool during the
turn. Channel adapters (e.g. whatsapp-gateway) MUST route this to
the owner's address (e.g. OWNER_JID) and NOT to the source chat.
Adapters that don't support owner-side delivery should ignore it
(BC-01 — Telegram/Discord/Slack continue to function unchanged).

thinking.description:
Combined thinking/reasoning trace from the model, when the caller
requested `show_thinking = true`.

MigrateScanRequest.description: Request to scan a directory for migration.

ParticipantRefSchema.description:
OpenAPI schema stand-in for `librefang_channels::types::ParticipantRef`.

The real type lives in `librefang-channels`, which does not depend on
utoipa. This mirror struct exists only so `#[schema(value_type = ...)]`
on `MessageRequest.group_participants` can expose the shape to the
generated OpenAPI document.

display_name.description: Human-readable name (push-name, contact name, or first part of JID).

PatchAgentConfigRequest.description: Request body for patching agent config (name, description, prompt, identity, model).

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens for LLM response. Controls conversation window size.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (0.0–2.0). Lower values are more deterministic.

PushMessageRequest.description: Request to push a proactive outbound message from an agent to a channel.

recipient.description: Recipient identifier (platform-specific: chat_id, username, email, etc.).

thread_id.description: Optional thread/topic ID for threaded replies (platform-specific).

RefreshRequest.description: Request body for the refresh token endpoint.

provider.description: Optional provider hint (if the user logged in with a specific provider).

refresh_token.description:
The refresh token obtained from the initial login callback.
If omitted, the server looks up a stored refresh token from the token store.

RotateKeyResponse.description:
Response payload for `POST /api/users/{name}/rotate-key`.

`new_api_key` is the **plaintext** rotated key — this is the only time
the server will surface it. Operators must copy and store it now;
nothing else (audit log included) records the plaintext.

`sessions_invalidated` reports how many in-memory per-user API key
records were swapped — typically `1`. See the doc-comment on
[`rotate_user_key`] for why dashboard sessions are NOT included in
this count.

SetAgentFileRequest.description: Request body for writing a workspace identity file.

SetAgentToolsRequest.description: Request body for updating an agent's tool configuration.

tool_allowlist.description: Tool allowlist — additional filter. `None` = no change, `Some([])` = clear.

tool_blocklist.description: Tool blocklist — exclusion filter. `None` = no change, `Some([])` = clear.

SetModeRequest.description: Request to change an agent's operational mode.

SkillInstallRequest.description: Request to install a skill from the marketplace.

hand.description: Install into a specific hand's workspace instead of globally.

SpawnRequest.description: Request to spawn an agent from a TOML manifest string or a template name.

manifest_toml.description: Agent manifest as TOML string (optional if `template` is provided).

name.description:
Optional custom name for the agent. Overrides the name from the template
or manifest, allowing multiple agents from the same template.

signed_manifest.description:
Optional Ed25519 signed manifest envelope (JSON).
When present, the signature is verified before spawning.

template.description:
Template name from `~/.librefang/workspaces/agents/{template}/agent.toml`.
When provided and `manifest_toml` is empty, the template is loaded automatically.

UpdateIdentityRequest.description: Request body for updating agent visual identity.

UserUpsert.description:
Payload for creating or replacing a user. `api_key_hash` is accepted
pre-hashed (Argon2 phc string) — the dashboard hashes locally before
sending. `None` clears any existing hash on update; absent on create.

UserView.description:
Sanitized user view returned over the wire — never echoes the
`api_key_hash` value, nor the contents of `tool_policy`,
`memory_access`, `budget`, etc. The list view only needs presence
flags so the dashboard can show a "this user is policy-customized"
badge; the per-user detail endpoints already surface the bodies.

has_budget.description: True when the user has a per-user budget cap configured.

has_memory_access.description: True when the user has a custom memory namespace ACL.

has_policy.description:
True when the user has any per-user tool policy configured —
either an allow/deny list, tool-category overrides, or
per-channel rules. Summary only; the contents stay behind
`/api/users/{name}/policy`.
